what_it_does: Defines an inline SVG outline icon showing two overlapping chat/message bubbles.
observations: Uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with no fill and currentColor stroke.
Sets stroke width to 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Contains two path elements, each drawing a speech-bubble shape.
The rear bubble includes a small tail near the lower left; the front bubble includes a tail pointing downward.
standouts: Layered two-bubble composition communicates multiple chats or conversation threads.
Color is inherited through currentColor, making the icon themeable.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon SVG conventions: fill="none", stroke="currentColor", rounded stroke styling, and a 24x24 coordinate system.
Uses compact path data in a single-line SVG.
Does not include title, desc, role, or aria attributes in the standalone SVG.
concerns: Minified single-line markup is harder to manually inspect or edit.
No embedded accessibility label is present if used directly as standalone content.
notes: A themeable outline SVG for representing multiple chat messages.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component or embedded in the SVG?
Is the overlapping bubble geometry intended to remain transparent where paths intersect?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies a double chat icon, and the SVG depicts two overlapping chat bubbles.
intention_signals: It appears intended to serve as a reusable interface icon for chats, conversations, or multi-message states while inheriting color from its context.
contribution: Contributes a compact, themeable, stroke-based double-chat icon with clear layered message-bubble geometry for UI surfaces that need a conversation or messaging glyph.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor outline SVG icon made from two paths depicting overlapping chat bubbles.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for a reusable design-system icon representing chats, conversation threads, or multiple messages.
